meaning: n.笑剧,闹剧,滑稽剧,胡闹vt.<古>填塞,为(演讲,作品等)加穿插
examples: The story has elements of tragedy and farce.
It's when you realize this is the sort of idiocy farce that this law falls apart.
The plot often borders on farce.
For the Magic organization, the damage was done long before this personnel farce unfolded during 2011-12.
The picture gently but surely moves back and forth between romantic comedy and satirical farce.
